text: vitamin A, riboflavin, vitamin B 12 Meat, fish, poultry B-complex vitamins, iron, iodine, fat PROTECTIVE Vitamins and Minerals FOODS Green leafy vegetables Antioxidants, fibre and other carotenoids Other vegetables and fruits Fibre, sugar and antioxidants Eggs, milk and milk products Protein and fat and flesh foods POINTS TO PONDER /c118Choose a variety of foods in amounts appropriate for age, gender, physiological status and physical activity. /c118Use a combination of whole grains, grams and greens. Include jaggery or sugar and cooking oils to bridge the calorie or energy gap. /c118Prefer fresh, locally available vegetables and fruits in plenty. /c118Include in the diets, foods of animal origin such as milk, eggs and meat, particularly for pregnant and lactating women and children.
